title: Expected number of hash collisions = (n-1)/2 (n keys, n slots)
statement_informal: Throwing n keys into n slots uniformly (load 1), the expected number of colliding pairs is (n-1)/2. Proved without enumerating the n^n assignments: appending a key adds count_eq v c collisions, and summing over the n slots gives |c| (each key matched once), so Tot_coll(S m) = n*Tot_coll m + m*n^m, hence 2*n*Tot_coll n k + k*n^k = k*k*n^k, and at k=n the mean is (n-1)/2.

cost_model.notes: coll counts colliding pairs. E = Tot_coll/(n^n). The pairwise expectation collapses (linearity over the last coordinate) to the running multiplicity: 2*n*Tot_coll n k + k*n^k = k*k*n^k, division-free; at k=n gives 2*Tot_coll n n = (n-1)*n^n, i.e. mean (n-1)/2.

references: Birthday/occupancy analysis: E[#collisions] = C(n,2)/m for n keys into m slots; load 1 gives (n-1)/2.
Verified twin of conjecture artifact tools/conjecture/results/hashing-collisions.json (enumerated E[collisions] = (n-1)/2).
